description: Progressive loading index - lightweight metadata for all skills with trigger examples for contextual matching

skills.name: onboarding
skills.description: Guide new users through a conversational discovery flow to create a personalized Claudia setup
examples: this is my first time using Claudia

skills.name: structure-generator
skills.description: Create personalized folder structures and files based on user archetype and workflow needs

skills.name: memory-manager
skills.description: Handle cross-session persistence using MCP tools from the claudia-memory server, with fallback to markdown files. Activates at session start to load context and session end to save learnings. Use when memory tools respond, fail, or are unavailable.
examples: what do you remember from last session?

skills.name: agent-dispatcher
skills.description: Detects when to delegate tasks to specialized agents with two-tier dispatch

skills.name: judgment-awareness
skills.description: Load and apply user-defined judgment rules from context/judgment.yaml to inform priority conflicts, escalation decisions, surfacing, and delegation
examples: which of these should I prioritize?

skills.name: commitment-detector
skills.description: Automatically detect promises and commitments in conversation and offer to track them
examples: I'll have the proposal to Sarah by Thursday
I told them I'd follow up next week
I need to send the contract before Friday

skills.name: pattern-recognizer
skills.description: Notice trends, recurring themes, and patterns across conversations and surface them when relevant. Activates when the same topic, frustration, or behavior appears across 3+ interactions.
examples: I've noticed you keep mentioning being overwhelmed
this is the third time this has come up
you tend to delay on these kinds of tasks

skills.name: relationship-tracker
skills.description: Surface relevant context when people are mentioned and track relationship health with velocity-based reconnection suggestions
examples: when did I last talk to Mike?

skills.name: risk-surfacer
skills.description: Proactively identify and surface potential problems using velocity-based relationship trends and commitment risk detection
examples: am I at risk of missing any deadlines?
what could go wrong this week?

skills.name: capability-suggester
skills.description: Notice repeated user behaviors and suggest new commands, workflows, structure, or integrations to streamline work. Also detects when the user's setup has outgrown its current structure and suggests targeted upgrades.
examples: you keep doing this manually, want me to automate it?
your setup might need an upgrade
I noticed a gap in your workflow

skills.name: hire-agent
skills.description: Suggests new agents based on repeated task patterns
examples: I keep delegating this same type of work
could a new agent handle this?

skills.name: vault-awareness
skills.description: Manage Obsidian vault projection with syncing, bidirectional edits, canvas generation, and vault-to-memory import
examples: generate a canvas for this project
update the vault with recent changes

skills.name: morning-brief
skills.description: Daily digest organized by urgency: overdue commitments, upcoming deadlines, reconnection suggestions, and recent changes. Activates at the start of the day or when user asks about their agenda.
examples: what should I focus on today?

skills.name: client-health
skills.description: Health check across active client engagements showing status, deliverables, and concerns
examples: any client issues I should know about?
show me client health across the board

skills.name: financial-snapshot
skills.description: Quick view of revenue, expenses, invoicing, and cash flow with key metrics

skills.name: growth-check
skills.description: Periodic reflection on development, skills, learning, and progress toward goals
examples: am I making progress on my goals?
what skills have I improved recently?

skills.name: databases
skills.description: View all Claudia memory databases, switch between them, manage isolation

skills.name: brain-monitor
skills.description: Terminal dashboard for real-time memory stats and activity
examples: what's happening in my memory system?

skills.name: diagnose
skills.description: Check memory system health and troubleshoot connectivity issues. Use when memory commands aren't working, at session start if something seems wrong, or when user asks about memory status.
examples: something seems wrong with the memory system

skills.name: inbox-check
skills.description: Lightweight inbox triage across all configured email accounts with two-tier fetch and judgment. Use when user asks about their email.

skills.name: meeting-prep
skills.description: One-page briefing before a call or meeting with person context, open items, and talking points. Use when user is preparing for a meeting or call.
examples: prep me for my call with Sarah
I have a meeting with Acme Corp in an hour
what should I know before talking to Mike?
meeting prep for the board call

skills.name: capture-meeting
skills.description: Process meeting notes or transcript to extract decisions, commitments, insights, and relationship updates. Use when user shares notes from a call or meeting.
examples: here are my notes from the call with Sarah
I just had a call, here's what happened
here's the transcript from today's meeting

skills.name: summarize-doc
skills.description: Create an executive summary of any document at the appropriate level of detail
examples: give me the gist of this

skills.name: memory-audit
skills.description: Show everything Claudia knows with provenance tracing and entity counts
examples: what do you know about me?
show me everything you remember about Sarah
what do you know about the Acme project?

skills.name: brain
skills.description: Launch the 3D Brain Visualizer showing entities, relationships, and memories as an interactive graph
examples: I want to see the 3D brain

skills.name: fix-duplicates
skills.description: Find and merge duplicate entities in the memory system
examples: I think you have duplicates for Sarah

skills.name: memory-health
skills.description: Check memory system health and data quality with stats and recommendations
examples: how healthy is my memory system?
how many memories do I have?

skills.name: file-document
skills.description: Store a document, email, or text for future reference with entity linking and provenance

skills.name: new-person
skills.description: Create a relationship tracking file for a person with contact info, history, and communication preferences. Use when user mentions someone important who doesn't have a file yet.
examples: add Sarah Chen as a new person
create a file for Mike from Acme
I want to track this person
new contact: James Wright, VP at TechCorp

skills.name: new-workspace
skills.description: Create a workspace skeleton for a new project, client, or venture with populated templates and dashboard. Use when starting a new engagement or project.
examples: create a workspace for the Acme project
I'm starting a new project, set up a workspace
new workspace for my consulting engagement

skills.name: connector-discovery
skills.description: Help users connect external tools in a gentle, non-technical way with plain language guidance
examples: how do I connect my calendar?
I want to integrate with Slack
can you access my Google Drive?

skills.name: research
skills.description: Deep research on a topic with web sources, memory integration, and stored findings. Also handles quick context-aware lookups when current information would improve the conversation.
examples: look into the latest pricing for Vercel
what's new with React Server Components?
any updates on that API deprecation?
check the current status of the bill

skills.name: what-am-i-missing
skills.description: Surface risks, blind spots, overlooked items, and accountability across commitments and relationships
examples: any blind spots I should know about?

skills.name: map-connections
skills.description: Scan context files to extract entities and relationships into the memory system
examples: who knows who in my contacts?

skills.name: weekly-review
skills.description: Guided weekly reflection across all relationships, commitments, and projects with pattern analysis. Use at end of week for structured reflection.

skills.name: meditate
skills.description: End-of-session reflection generating persistent learnings about user preferences, communication patterns, and cross-session insights. Also extracts judgment rules from decisions made during the session.
examples: what did you learn from this conversation?

skills.name: draft-reply
skills.description: Draft an email response with tone matching the relationship context. Shows draft for approval before sending.
examples: draft a reply to Sarah's email
help me respond to this message
write a response to the client
reply to this email for me

skills.name: follow-up-draft
skills.description: Create a post-meeting thank-you or follow-up email with key points and next steps. Use after a meeting capture when next steps need communicating.
examples: draft a follow-up to my meeting with Sarah
write a thank you note for the call
follow up with the key points from today

skills.name: ingest-sources
skills.description: Process multiple source documents with Extract-Then-Aggregate discipline. Use when user shares multiple transcripts, emails, or documents for batch processing.
examples: process all the transcripts in this folder
I have 5 meeting notes to ingest

skills.name: pipeline-review
skills.description: Review active pipeline, opportunities, capacity, and stalled items needing attention
examples: what's the status of my deals?

skills.name: deep-context
skills.description: Full-context deep analysis using memory.deep_context compound tool (single call replaces 6-8 sequential calls) for comprehensive synthesis
examples: give me the full picture on this
deep dive into the Acme relationship
everything you know about this project
